test_cases.input: Story: Investigating chemical exposure at a residential housing development built on former industrial land. Publication: regional investigative news outlet. No experts yet. 6-week deadline.
test_cases.notes: Domains were well-separated: toxicology, environmental law, EPA enforcement, and affected residents. Finding strategies for academic toxicologists and EPA alumni were specific and realistic.

test_cases.input: Story: Investigating gender pay gap at a regional university. Publication: national higher education journal. One expert already identified: an HR consultant. Tight 3-week deadline.
test_cases.notes: Brief correctly flagged that HR consultants hired by institutions have inherent conflicts. Finding strategy for labor economists was practical. Approach notes were realistic about faculty willingness to criticize own institution.

test_cases.input: Story: Cybersecurity breach at a state government agency exposing citizen data. Publication: technology and policy magazine. No experts identified. Standard deadline.
test_cases.notes: Domains covered technical forensics, state IT policy, and legal liability. Sourcing risks section correctly warned about vendor NDAs restricting forensics firms. Actionability was slightly lower — some finding strategies were generic.

editorial_naturalness_baseline.protocol: EVAL_FRAMEWORK.md Section A — Editorial Naturalness rubric (1-5 anchored on observable lexical, structural, tonal, and genre tells).
editorial_naturalness_baseline.calibration_methodology: Single-output baseline pass scoring the in-skill canonical example (the dimension reviewer's primary calibration target per the AUT-23 SKILL_TEMPLATE.md update).
editorial_naturalness_baseline.tells_observed: None. Imperative voice is direct and operational ('Come with data,' 'Lead with the systemic question, not the specific hospital, to lower defensiveness').
editorial_naturalness_baseline.strongest_genre_craft: Working editor's voice — short sentences, named platforms, specific search filters. The Sourcing Risks section names real failure modes ('advocacy capture,' 'overreliance on government sources') without abstracting them. Reads like a memo from a senior investigations editor briefing a junior reporter.
editorial_naturalness_baseline.note_on_pre_existing_fields: The overall_mean, dimension_means, and per-test-case scores below are preserved as a record of the original eval session and were never updated by the AUT-7 actionability relift. The eval_score in the skill frontmatter and EVAL_SUMMARY.md table reflect post-AUT-7 + post-AUT-23 values; this baseline block is the new authoritative source for those numbers.
